text.title: LLMJudge — LLM-as-Judge Scoring and Pairwise Comparison
text.summary: LLMJudge class for single-output scoring (1-5 per criterion) and pairwise A/B comparison with position-bias mitigation.

rule.statement: Use a more capable model as judge than the model being evaluated (e.g., judge gpt-4o-mini outputs with gpt-4o).
rule.rationale: Evaluating with the same model introduces self-preference bias; a stronger model produces more calibrated scores.

rule.statement: Include the word "JSON" in the prompt when using response_format=json_object; the API raises an error if it is absent.
rule.rationale: OpenAI enforces this requirement at the API level; silent omission causes evaluation failures.

rule.statement: Wrap json.loads() in try/except json.JSONDecodeError and return a default error dict on failure.
rule.rationale: Judge models occasionally produce malformed JSON; uncaught errors abort the entire batch evaluation.

example.note: json.JSONDecodeError is caught explicitly. "JSON" appears in the prompt for response_format compliance.

rule.statement: Run pairwise comparisons in both orderings (A vs B and B vs A) and average the results.
rule.rationale: Position bias causes the first-listed response to be preferred ~55-60% of the time; averaging both orderings removes this systematic error.

antipattern.description: Running pairwise comparison with only one ordering (A always first) and using the winner directly.
antipattern.reason: Position bias produces a false winner ~5-10% of the time, making model selection unreliable.

rule.statement: Parallelize LLMJudge.evaluate_batch with asyncio or concurrent.futures for batches larger than 20 cases.
rule.rationale: Sequential API calls at 1s latency each make large batches impractical; parallelization reduces wall-clock time proportionally.

rule.statement: Cap LLM judge evaluation at 50-100 examples for routine CI checks; reserve full-batch eval for major releases.
rule.rationale: Each judge call costs money; routine checks at sample size 50-100 provide sufficient signal without large spend.

rule.statement: Do not autonomously block production deployments based solely on LLM judge scores — require human review of threshold calibration.
rule.rationale: LLM-as-judge is a proxy metric; threshold calibration requires human validation before it can be treated as a gate.

text.reference: Prompt templates for single scoring and pairwise comparison
